sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 5.44 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.5)

statement.latex: A union${ A}= _{ } { V}_{ }$of varieties of groups (in the lattice of varieties) is called irreducible\/ if$_{ } { V}_{ } { A}$for each index$$. Is every variety an irreducible union of (finitely or infinitely many) varieties each of which cannot be decomposed into a union of two proper subvarieties? }

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
